info.name: Mamsa API (v1)
info.description: Mamsa platform REST API — Laravel Sanctum. Auth endpoints auto-capture access_token/refresh_token into collection variables; create endpoints capture their IDs. Set {{base_url}} and run Auth → (Verify OTP / Admin Login) first.

request.description:
Without `intent` this is the legacy unified passwordless flow (verify-otp will create the user row if missing). Send `intent: "login" | "register"` to get an early 422 with a machine-readable `code` instead (see the two intent variants below).

Non-production returns `debug_otp` in the response and this request auto-captures it into {{otp_code}} (so Verify OTP just works). When OTP_FIXED_CODE is set (e.g. staging), every OTP is that fixed value — **111222**. Production sends a real SMS and omits debug_otp.

Explicit **login**: if the phone is NOT registered (no user row, or profile never completed — blank name), responds `422` with:

```json
{ "success": false, "message": "هذا الرقم غير مسجّل", "code": "PHONE_NOT_REGISTERED" }
```

…before any SMS is sent or user row created. Registered phone → normal 200 + OTP.

Explicit **register**: if the phone is ALREADY registered (profile completed), responds `422` with:

```json
{ "success": false, "message": "هذا الرقم مسجّل بالفعل، يرجى تسجيل الدخول", "code": "PHONE_ALREADY_REGISTERED" }
```

A row with a blank name (abandoned sign-in) counts as unregistered, so it can finish registering → normal 200 + OTP.

Captures access_token + refresh_token into collection variables on success. Code = {{otp_code}} (auto-filled by Request OTP from debug_otp). With OTP_FIXED_CODE set in non-production it's the fixed value — **111222**.
Also accepts the same optional `intent: "login" | "register"` guard as Request OTP (422 + PHONE_NOT_REGISTERED / PHONE_ALREADY_REGISTERED). Throttled 3/min.
type=individual requires national_id; type=company requires cr_number.
Send/resend partner email verification code (FR-005). Auth required.
Verify partner email (FR-005). Auth required.
Public catalogue search. Filters: q, city, type, category, min_price, max_price, capacity, bedrooms, min_rating (avg review score), features[] (amenity name, repeatable; AND semantics).
Public list of a unit's reviews.

Public availability export — paste this URL into Airbnb/Booking.com so they block Mamsa's confirmed bookings + manual closures.

No auth: the unguessable 40–80 char `calendar_token` **is** the credential (run *Partner → Calendar → Get Unit Calendar* first to capture it). Returns `text/calendar` VCALENDAR. Throttled 60 req/min.
Public homepage testimonials (آراء عملائنا).
Step 1: send OTP to the new phone.
Step 2: verify OTP and switch the phone.
Soft-delete: scrub PII, revoke tokens. Returns 204.
Saved cards store metadata only (brand/last4/expiry) — never the full PAN. First card added becomes the default.
[{ id, brand, last4, exp_month, exp_year, is_default }] — brand ∈ visa|mastercard|mada. Default first.
Marks this card the default; clears the flag on the others. Returns 204.
Removes a card. If it was the default, another is promoted. Returns 204.
[{ id, ref_code, type, amount, description, date, status }] — type ∈ payment|refund|topup|reward; amount signed; status ∈ completed|pending|failed.
The user's favourited units (supported types, approved & available).
Idempotent add under the (user, unit) unique index. Returns 204.
Removes the unit from favourites. Returns 204.

Returns the full booking incl. itemised "pricing" (nightly_rate, subtotal, service_fee, cleaning_fee, taxes, total) and, when cancelled, a "cancellation" block (reason, cancelled_by_label, refunded_amount). Powers the booking-detail page.
FR-043/044 — read-only refund quote from the frozen snapshot.
FR-046/047 — runs the automatic refund/void. Optional "reason" (max 500) is stored and shown on the cancelled-booking card.
token = Moyasar.js card token (real mode). Leave blank in test mode.
Public webhook — Moyasar posts payment status here.
Editing an approved unit sends it back to Pending (FR-066).
Multipart upload (1–10 files). mimes: jpg,png,webp,avif; max 5MB each. First photo on an empty gallery becomes main. Stored on the public disk under units/{id}/…

exec: if (Array.isArray(d) && d[0] && d[0].id != null) pm.collectionVariables.set('image_id', d[0].id);

request.description: Marks this image the main one; clears the flag on the rest.
Removes the photo + its file. If it was main, another is promoted.

item.name: Calendar
item.description: Availability calendar (anti double-booking): manual closures + two-way iCal sync. All owner-scoped (403 otherwise).

request.description:
Settings + closures + booked ranges for one unit (owner only).

Response: `export_url` (public .ics feed for Airbnb/Booking), `ical_import_url`, `ical_synced_at`, `blocked_dates[]` (id, start_date, end_date, source: manual|ical, note), `booked[]` (read-only pending/confirmed bookings so the UI can paint them).

Lazily mints `calendar_token` for units created before the feature existed.

Save the external feed URL (Booking/Airbnb export). The backend fetches and parses the feed **before** saving — an unreadable/non-iCal URL returns **422**.

On success it runs the first sync immediately (no 15-min wait) and returns the same payload as Get Unit Calendar.

Send `"ical_import_url": null` to disable: deletes all source=ical blocks and clears `ical_synced_at`.

Manual closure (maintenance, external booking…). Validation: `start_date` >= today, `end_date` strictly after `start_date`, `note` optional (max 255).

**201** returns `{id, start_date, end_date, source: "manual", note}`. Blocked ranges are enforced in availability + booking creation.

Delete a **manual** closure → **204**.

source=ical rows return **422** — they mirror the external feed and would be resurrected on the next sync; remove them on the source platform instead.
Partner join applications. status=pending|approved|rejected (omit for all); search matches name/phone. Response rows include user_id — use it as {{user_id}} for approve/reject.

THE partner-approval action (FR flow): sets partnerDetail.status=approved + reviewed_at and notifies the applicant (in-app + email). After this the partner can log into partner.mamsaa.com. NOT the same as Users/Update Status (is_active) — that only activates/suspends the account and does NOT clear ACCOUNT_PENDING. 422 if the application is not pending (already approved/rejected).
Rejects the application with a required reason (max 500); notifies the applicant. A rejected applicant can re-submit partner/register, which returns them to pending.
Public contact form (§9).

item.name: 11 — Partner Dashboard (contract API)
Auth (OTP → session cookie)

request.description: 9-digit phone starting with 5 (no leading 0). Staging uses fixed OTP 111222; the code is never returned.
Same as Request OTP (rate-limited by pd-otp). Re-sends the login code; daily cap 10/phone.

On success sets the httpOnly `mamsa-session` cookie — Postman's cookie jar stores it and sends it on every later dashboard request automatically. Gate errors: PARTNER_NOT_FOUND / ACCOUNT_PENDING / ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED / OTP_WRONG / OTP_EXPIRED / OTP_LOCKED.
Invalidates the session + clears the cookie.

item.description: Cookie-session auth (NOT bearer). Run Verify OTP once; the cookie jar carries the session through the rest of this folder.

request.description: Partner profile + accountState/hostCancellationsLast12m/flagged.
Only name + email are editable here.
Sends OTP to the NEW number.
Company payout docs + server-computed `complete`. N/A for individuals.
cr=10 digits, iban=SA+22 digits; file ids come from Uploads.
Home cards + 12-month series. totalRevenue/thisMonthRevenue are PARTNER SHARE (after 2%). unitsCount excludes drafts.
Filters: status=draft|pending|approved|rejected, q (name/code). Paginated {data, meta}.
Creates a DRAFT (partial bodies allowed). Captures the new id into {{unit_id}}. city must be an enum slug; amenities are the 8 contract keys.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "وحدة من بوستمان",
  "type": "apartment",
  "pricePerNight": 420,
  "capacity": 4,
  "bedrooms": 2,
  "bathrooms": 2,
  "city": "riyadh",
  "district": "العليا",
  "description": "وصف تجريبي لوحدة أنشئت من Postman لأغراض الاختبار.",
  "amenities": [
    "wifi",
    "ac",
    "kitchen"
  ],
  "checkIn": "15:00",
  "checkOut": "12:00",
  "lat": 24.7136,
  "lng": 46.6753,
  "address": "حي العليا، الرياض",
  "tourismLicenseNumber": "TL-PM-0001",
  "tourismLicenseFileId": "{{file_id}}"
}

exec: const id = (j && j.id) ? j.id : (j && j.data && j.data.id);

request.description: Editing an APPROVED unit auto-reverts it to pending. Blocked (409) while pending.
draft/rejected → pending. Full validation (Saudi-bounds lat/lng, city enum, license, ≥1 photo, company-docs gate). Returns { unit, message }.
Drafts only; others → 409 UNIT_NOT_DELETABLE.
Bare array of days. status: available|booked|external|blocked (priority booked>external>blocked>available).
Reason optional. Any booked/external day in the list → 409 DATE_UNAVAILABLE.
Manually-blocked days only.
Bare array of { id, source, url, status, lastSync }.
Validates the URL fetches valid iCal before saving (else 400 INVALID_ICAL); syncs immediately. Captures {{feed_id}}.

exec: const id = (j && j.id) ? j.id : (j && j.data && j.data.id);

request.description: Returns { url } — a public tokenized .ics under /api/v1/calendar/{token}.ics. Don't build it yourself.

item.name: Bookings

request.description: Filters: status, unitId, from/to (date range), q (booking code). Paginated.
Atomic + idempotent (send Idempotency-Key). 100% refund to guest, dates blocked, guest SMS, host_cancellation notification. Preconditions (else 409): status=confirmed & check-in in the future.

item.description: Contract §6. Bookings are created by the user website — dashboard only reads + host-cancels.

request.description: grossRevenue (full totals), commission (2%), netProfit, series, perUnit. Both from/to required, to>=from.
format=pdf → real Arabic RTL PDF (mpdf). format=csv|xlsx → CSV download. Binary — use Postman 'Send and Download'.

format=csv|xlsx → UTF-8-BOM CSV download (xlsx aliases to CSV). Columns: Code, Unit, Guest, Check-in, Check-out, Nights, Total (SAR), Commission, Net, Status. Binary — use Postman 'Send and Download'.

item.name: Notifications

request.description: Newest first: { id, type, title, body, read, createdAt, href }. Paginated.
{ count }.

item.description: Contract §8. 5 types: unit_approved, unit_rejected, new_booking, sync_failed, host_cancellation.

item.name: Uploads

request.description: kind: unit_photo|license_pdf|company_doc. Returns { uploadUrl, fileId }; PUT the raw bytes to uploadUrl (validated by magic bytes + size ≤10MB), then reference fileId. Captures {{file_id}} + {{upload_url}}.

Step 2 of the upload flow: PUT the RAW file bytes to the {{upload_url}} captured from Presign. Auth is the URL signature (no session). Set the Body to 'binary' and pick a file whose type matches the presign kind (unit_photo=jpeg/png, license_pdf/company_doc=pdf) — magic bytes + size ≤10MB are validated. Then reference {{file_id}} in the unit/company-docs payload.

item.description: Contract §9.1. Signed PUT to our own endpoint (no S3).

request.description: Moyasar → Mamsa payment/refund callback. Verified by a shared secret token in the controller, NOT session auth — the frontend never calls this; included for completeness. Body below is a sample event.

item.description: Root-mounted contract API for the Next.js partner dashboard (base = {{dashboard_url}}, NO /api/v1). Cookie-session auth: run Auth → Verify OTP first; the cookie jar carries the session. Error envelope { error:{code,message,fields} }. See docs/NEXTJS-DASHBOARD-*.md.
